existing_claim: Attacked the remedy-boundary decision for scope overreach — whether it smuggles supersession/retraction mechanics that belong upstream. It held: §6.1 decides only the replaceability-first *policy test* and explicitly cites the landed schema mechanics (DDR-002 §7 #21, #22; SDD-001 §3.5.3–§3.5.4) as executing, not re-authored; §6.2 decides the re-admission *policy* and points to DDR-002 §5 for the read-exclusion mechanism. The decision stays at policy altitude and names its dependencies; consequences (durable determination content) are declared.
incoming_claim: Attacked the remedy-boundary decision from the LAA altitude for scope overreach — whether it smuggles supersession/retraction mechanics that belong to the schema or upstream. It held: §6.1 decides only the replaceability-first / conjunctive-retraction *policy test*, records the defect-at-promotion-vs-world-moved axis as informing-only ('never decides the remedy'), and explicitly cites the landed mechanics (DDR-002 §7 #21/#22; SDD-001 §3.5.3–§3.5.4) as executing, not re-authored; §6.2 decides the re-admission *policy* (forward-act only) and keys read-exclusion on the executed RETRACTS edge 'exactly as landed' per DDR-002 §5. The decision stays at policy altitude, names its dependencies, and declares its durable-determination consequences.

existing_claim: Attacked the record for smuggled or undeclared commitments — decisions riding along without being surfaced. It held for the deferral surface: §9 declares each in-scope-but-unlocked item with a named owner and reversal path, and each is consistent with a Decision-block or body reference. The record's dependencies (candidate shape, structured diagnosis capture, executed-retraction analogue, decision-consumed survival mechanism) are named and routed rather than quietly assumed decided.
incoming_claim: Attacked the record for smuggled commitments — decisions riding along without being surfaced as gaps. It held for the deferral surface: §9 declares each in-scope-but-unlocked item (candidate-ObservedPattern shape, structured diagnosis capture, decision-consumed survival mechanism, multi-condition composition, calibration values, access-model surface) with a named owner and reversal path, and each is consistent with a body reference (§5.1, §2.2, §7.2, §6.3, §4). Dependencies are named and routed rather than quietly assumed decided — the record does not claim to have settled what it defers.

existing_claim: Attacked the remedy boundary as a candidate premature or under-authorized commitment: at n=0 wrong promotions, does DDR-003 over-commit a mechanism that belongs in its own decision, or pull against ADR-008's routing? It held. ADR-008 §2.6 explicitly routes the remedy boundary (supersede vs retract) to this record by name; the replaceability-first/conjunctive-retraction test is form-fixed on qualitative grounds with constants held, and §6.2's forward-act rule integrates cleanly with the landed RETRACTS read-exclusion (DDR-002 §5) without re-opening it. The reversibility posture is proportionate — the boundary is stated, remedies ride landed EA-gated mechanics, and the executed-retraction detection residue is honestly deferred. This is the record's authorized centerpiece standing in its correct shape at the correct time.
incoming_claim: Attacked the remedy boundary as a premature or over-authorized commitment: at n=0 wrong promotions, does DDR-003 over-commit a mechanism, pull against ADR-008's routing, or take a reversibility cost the platform posture should not yet bear? It held. ADR-008 §2.6 routes the remedy boundary (supersede-vs-retract) to this record by name; §6.1's replaceability-first/conjunctive-retraction test is form-fixed on qualitative grounds with constants held, records the defect-at-promotion-vs-world-moved axis as informing-only ('never decides the remedy'), and executes on landed EA-gated mechanics (DDR-002 §7 #21/#22, SDD-001 §3.5.3–§3.5.4) without re-authoring them. §6.2's forward-act rule keys re-admission on the landed executed RETRACTS edge and routes un-retraction through a fresh forward gate — integrating with the ratified read-exclusion (DDR-002 §5) without re-opening it, and honestly deferring the executed-retraction detection residue to §9. The commitment stands in its correct shape, at its correct altitude, at the correct time.

existing_claim: Attacked the mechanism-now/values-deferred cut as a dodge that ships an ungoverned loop under an empirical-floor banner. It held. Every reversibility-expensive surface (the two-controls seam, diagnosis dimensions, role split, no-leak invariant, retention-class model, audit posture) is ruled now; every calibration value is structured into one EA-owned, audit-logged, fresh-read mechanism with two-tier governance adopted by pointer (DDR-004 §6) and a real coherence invariant (§4.3 retention ≥ lookback) that prevents a silent detection-degradation failure. The cut is disciplined rather than evasive — form fixed, constants honestly held with named owners, no premature calibration asserted. Posture is proportionate to the n=0 floor.
incoming_claim: Attacked the mechanism-now/values-deferred cut as a posture dodge — shipping an ungoverned loop under an empirical-floor banner, or committing structure prematurely against zero traffic. It held as proportionate to the n=0 floor. Every reversibility-expensive surface (the two-controls seam, diagnosis dimensions, role split, no-leak invariant, retention-class model, audit posture) is ruled now; every calibration value routes into one EA-owned, audit-logged, fresh-read mechanism whose two-tier governance is adopted by pointer (DDR-004 §6) rather than re-authored, and whose §4.3 retention≥lookback coherence invariant prevents a silent detection-degradation failure. No calibration value is asserted; each is a named gap with an owner. The cut integrates with the corpus's ratified mechanism-now/values-at-onboarding discipline and pulls against nothing upstream.

The §7.1 preamble scopes the model as '(Reasoning Graph, role-based, non-uniform).' The decision as stated does not decide what its own body supports.

existing_claim: §4 lists 'working-tier retention windows (§7.1)' as a single named calibration parameter and §4.3 validates 'every §7.1 working-tier retention window' against lookbacks, implying multiple windows. §7.1 class (3) states 'bounded retention windows, values deferred (§4)' — plural — while §4 names one parameter. Additionally §7.2 states class-2 Evidence 'is exempt from expiry until a survival mechanism ... lands' — whether that exemption is itself a §4 parameter or a fixed interim is not consistently declared between §4's parameter set and §7.2. The retention-parameter decision is stated at differing cardinality and scope across §4 and §7, so a reader cannot determine what the §4 mechanism actually governs.
incoming_claim: §4 lists 'working-tier retention windows (§7.1)' as a single named calibration parameter, while §4.3 validates 'every §7.1 working-tier retention window' against lookbacks — implying multiple. §7.1 class (3) states 'bounded retention windows, values deferred (§4)' (plural). Additionally §7.2 states class-2 Evidence 'is exempt from expiry ... until a survival mechanism ... lands' — whether that exemption is itself a §4 parameter or a fixed interim is not stated in §4's parameter set. The retention-parameter decision is stated at differing cardinality and scope across §4 and §7, so a reader cannot determine what the §4 mechanism actually governs.

But DDR-002 v1.6.0 (in this record's own reference set) still pins ADR-002 v1.1.0 and its Change Log routes the ADR-002 v1.1.0→v1.2.0 currency cascade to a separate open ticket (RBT-66) as not-yet-folded; DDR-004 v1.4.0 likewise pins ADR-002 v1.1.0 ('the ADR-002 v1.1.0→v1.2.0 currency ... is a separate ... cascade tracked on RBT-66, not folded here'); SDD-001 v1.5.0 pins ADR-002 v1.1.0 with the same deferral. A new record entering the corpus pinned at ADR-002 v1.2.0 while every cited sibling sits at v1.1.0 pending an explicitly-unlanded cross-record cascade commits the set to a pin state ahead of a decision the corpus has not yet ratified across records — the reversibility question is whether DDR-003 should stand at v1.2.0 at this time or await the RBT-66 cascade.

existing_claim: §5.1 commits that 'until that shape and the review surface land, no conforming distillation write path into the Operational plane exists — a distillation writer designed before then fails ADR-008 §6's accountability check at design review.' This is a hard-to-reverse gating commitment: a PROPOSED record, at n=0 distillations, forecloses all Operational-plane distillation work until two forthcoming artifacts (the candidate-shape schema amendment and the review surface) — both routed to the still-unauthored detection-promotion service design — exist. DDR-001 v1.5.0 already ratifies the Operational plane as a live durable-distilled-ObservedPattern data-path, and DDR-002 §2.3 carries the distillation write as a live ratified schema shape whose checkpoint posture is 'explicitly un-decided.' Whether a downstream-blocking dependency of this weight — gating a plane's write path on an unauthored sibling record's deliverables — belongs in this record's decision at this time, or is a premature commitment made ahead of the detection-promotion design's own authoring, deserves an explicit posture judgment rather than a body-section consequence.
incoming_claim: §5.1 commits that 'until that shape and the review surface land, no conforming distillation write path into the Operational plane exists — a distillation writer designed before then fails ADR-008 §6's accountability check at design review.' This is a hard-to-reverse gating commitment: a PROPOSED record, at n=0 distillations, forecloses all Operational-plane distillation work until two forthcoming artifacts (the candidate-shape schema amendment and the review surface) — both routed to the still-unauthored detection-promotion service design (§9) — exist. DDR-001 v1.5.0 ratifies the Operational plane as a live durable-distilled-ObservedPattern data-path, and DDR-002 §2.3 carries the distillation write as a live ratified schema shape whose checkpoint posture is 'explicitly un-decided.' Whether a downstream-blocking dependency of this weight — gating a plane's write path on an unauthored sibling record's deliverables — belongs in this record's decision at this time, or is a premature commitment made ahead of the detection-promotion design's own authoring, is a posture judgment that should be surfaced as an explicit consequence rather than carried in a body section; the reversibility cost of the foreclosure is not proportionate to a PROPOSED record against zero traffic.

existing_claim: §1.3 states 'ADR-008 §2.2 fixes both controls as *human* review with a named human answerable; any future non-human role-holder is therefore an amendment question for that upstream authority.' This pins a standing human-in-the-loop posture on the two governance controls. ADR-001 §2.1 commits the platform to a Position-4 trajectory (SOFIA-as-reasoner, LLM-as-tool, encoding density grows over time), and §2.5's encoding-density-growth mechanism is precisely the promotion of recurring reasoning into encoded logic that the promotion gate governs. Whether the human-only fence integrates with the committed direction of travel or is a reversibility cost against it is a posture question worth an explicit sentence; the record correctly routes the non-human-holder question upstream to ADR-008, so the fence is not itself unauthorized — but its relationship to the ratified Position-4 trajectory is stated only by omission.

The record correctly routes the non-human-holder question upstream to ADR-008, so the fence is not itself unauthorized — but whether the human-only fence integrates with the committed direction of travel or stands as a reversibility cost against it is a posture question stated only by omission. Its relationship to the ratified Position-4 trajectory deserves an explicit sentence rather than silence.

It held. ADR-008 §2.6 explicitly routes the remedy boundary (supersede vs retract) to this record by name; the replaceability-first/conjunctive-retraction test is form-fixed on qualitative grounds with constants held, and §6.2's forward-act rule integrates cleanly with the landed RETRACTS read-exclusion (DDR-002 §5) without re-opening it. The reversibility posture is proportionate — the boundary is stated, remedies ride landed EA-gated mechanics, and the executed-retraction detection residue is honestly deferred. This is the record's authorized centerpiece standing in its correct shape at the correct time.
incoming_claim: Attacked the remedy boundary as a candidate premature or over-authorized commitment: at n=0 wrong promotions, does DDR-003 over-commit a mechanism that belongs upstream, pull against ADR-008's routing, or take a reversibility cost the posture should not yet bear? It held. ADR-008 §2.6 routes the remedy boundary (supersede-vs-retract) to this record by name; §6.1's replaceability-first/conjunctive-retraction test is form-fixed on qualitative grounds with constants held, records the defect-at-promotion-vs-world-moved axis as informing-only ('never decides the remedy'), and executes on landed EA-gated mechanics (DDR-002 §7 #21/#22, SDD-001 §3.5.3–§3.5.4) without re-authoring them. §6.2's forward-act rule keys re-admission on the landed executed RETRACTS edge (DDR-002 §5) and routes un-retraction through a fresh forward gate, integrating with the ratified read-exclusion without re-opening it. The commitment stands in its correct shape, at its correct altitude, at the correct time.

existing_claim: §8 item 4 states re-admission forward-act-only 'holds by construction' because 'no consumption path evaluates governing verdicts at read time; read-exclusion keys on the executed `RETRACTS` edge.' But §6.2 concedes 'A standing detection analogue for executed retractions whose governing verdict is non-approving ... is a named gap (§9),' and §9 carries it as an unbuilt future amendment. The property rests on a reviewed forward-act discipline (human behavior enforced at design review — §8 marks the check 'safety-relevant; holds by construction'), not on a structural guarantee; the record's own text routes the missing structural check to a future instance. 'Holds by construction' overclaims what the current schema enforces.
incoming_claim: §8 item 4 states re-admission forward-act-only 'holds by construction' because 'no consumption path evaluates governing verdicts at read time; read-exclusion keys on the executed `RETRACTS` edge.' §6.2 concedes 'A standing detection analogue for executed retractions whose governing verdict is non-approving — the retraction-side mirror of the promoted-origin invariant (DDR-002 §7 #15) — is a named gap (§9); the forward-act rule is what makes that gap an audit-reconciliation concern rather than a consumption defect.' The property rests on a reviewed forward-act discipline (human behavior enforced at design review) plus the absence of any read-time verdict evaluation, not on a structural guarantee the current schema mechanizes; §9 routes the missing structural check to a future amendment. 'Holds by construction' overstates a guarantee that is partly behavioral and partly deferred.

existing_claim: DDR-003 References pins 'ADR-002 v1.2.0'. Its own referenced siblings disagree: DDR-004 v1.4.0 References pins 'ADR-002 v1.1.0' and its Change Log routes the ADR-002 v1.1.0→v1.2.0 currency cascade to RBT-66 as not-yet-folded; SDD-001 v1.5.0 References pins 'ADR-002 v1.1.0' and its §10 Change Log states the ADR-002 currency + §4.6 realignment is 'deliberately left as-is ... separate RBT-66 cascade ... not folded here.' A new record entering the corpus at ADR-002 v1.2.0 while its cited substrate siblings sit at v1.1.0 pending an explicitly unlanded cascade leaves a downstream reader unable to confirm DDR-003 §1.1's ADR-002 §2.6 citation and SDD-001's ADR-002 §2.6 citation reference the same amended text.
incoming_claim: DDR-003 References pins 'ADR-002 v1.2.0', and §1.1 / §6.1 / §6.4 lean load-bearingly on ADR-002 §2.6 (author/executor/authorizer separation) and §2.5 (cross-origin block). Its own referenced siblings disagree: DDR-004 v1.4.0 References pins 'ADR-002 v1.1.0' and its Change Log routes the ADR-002 v1.1.0→v1.2.0 currency cascade to RBT-66 as not-yet-folded; SDD-001 v1.5.0 References pins 'ADR-002 v1.1.0' with the same deferral ('deliberately left as-is ... separate RBT-66 cascade ... not folded here'). A downstream reader reconciling DDR-003 §1.1's ADR-002 §2.6 citation against SDD-001's ADR-002 v1.1.0 pin cannot confirm the same amended §2.6 text is referenced across the dependency chain the record cites.

existing_claim: §8 item 1 classes the non-bypassable gates as '(safety-relevant; mechanized)' and cites 'DDR-001 conformance check 4' as a mechanized non-bypassable gate. DDR-001's Conformance Checks section is headed 'Conformance Checks (aspirational; mechanization deferred)' and closes '*Aspirational checkpoints — mechanized enforcement is deferred, not CI-enforced at authoring.*'. DDR-002 §7 marks its own #9 as follow-tier read-discipline (proposal-visibility, gateway-enforced read-discipline + structural label-skip aid), not a landed mechanization. Labeling DDR-001 check 4 'mechanized' overstates the enforcement state the cited authority declares.
incoming_claim: §8 item 1 is annotated '(safety-relevant; aspirational — mechanization deferred per DDR-001)' and cites 'DDR-001 conformance check 4' as one of the landed gate invariants. DDR-001's Conformance Checks section is headed 'Conformance Checks (aspirational; mechanization deferred)' and closes '*Aspirational checkpoints — mechanized enforcement is deferred, not CI-enforced at authoring.*'; DDR-002 §7 marks its own #9 (proposal-visibility) as follow-tier gateway-enforced read-discipline plus a structural label-skip aid, not a landed mechanization. The item 1 phrasing that the gates 'ride the landed gate invariants' reads as more enforcement than the cited authorities declare.

existing_claim: §9 names the executed-retraction detection analogue as a DDR-003 named gap ('a future additive graph-schema amendment plus conformance-harness entry, at first real retraction instance'). DDR-002 v1.6.0 §2.4 carries the same item routed to 'DDR-003 remedy-boundary territory ... a standing detection analogue of #15 for executed retractions ... is not authored here,' and ADR-008 §5.3 carries it as its own named follow-up ('the detection mechanics are a named schema / governance follow-up'). Three records name the same schema gap as a deferral without one designating the canonical owner; at first real retraction instance it is unclear whose amendment opens it.
incoming_claim: §9 names the executed-retraction detection analogue as a DDR-003 named gap ('a future additive graph-schema amendment plus conformance-harness entry, at first real retraction instance'). DDR-002 v1.6.0 §2.4 carries the same item routed to 'DDR-003 remedy-boundary territory ... a standing detection analogue of #15 for executed retractions ... is not authored here,' and ADR-008 §5.3 carries it as its own named follow-up ('the detection mechanics are a named schema / governance follow-up'). Three records name the same schema gap as a deferral without one designating the canonical owner of the amendment act; at first real retraction instance it is unresolved whose amendment opens it.

existing_claim: Decision.10 enumerates the retention/audit ruling as a 'four-class, role-based model (§7.1) with two survival exemptions: decision-consumed Evidence and promotion-consumed substrate (§7.2)'. It does not surface that the decision-consumed-Evidence exemption is an interim posture resting on an unbuilt survival mechanism (§7.2: 'exempt from expiry until a survival mechanism ... lands', §9: 'made non-load-bearing by the §7.2 exemption interim'). The Decision block is the contract's index; a reader treating Decision.10 as complete would not learn that a survival exemption rests on a routed forward dependency.
